text: Residents on Oak Street (blocks 400-600) are reporting persistent poor air quality near the new Northgate mixed-use construction site. Dust and diesel exhaust visible most mornings. Two elderly residents with asthma have been hospitalized this month.
summary: Health-impacting construction emissions on Oak Street requiring immediate CEMP compliance review.
recommendation: Issue formal notice to general contractor to produce CEMP documentation. Require Tier 4 or electric equipment by next working day. Coordinate with Public Health on air quality monitoring deployment.

text: The new apartment complex at 1200 Riverside Drive was approved last year but they've cleared all the large oak trees on the site perimeter. I counted at least 8 mature trees removed without any posted permits.
summary: Potential unpermitted removal of 8+ protected trees at 1200 Riverside Drive development.
recommendation: Urban Forestry to conduct site inspection within 48 hours. If violation confirmed, issue stop-work order and require 3:1 replacement plan or Tree Canopy Fund contribution.

text: My neighborhood has 3 broken EV charging stations (City-owned, Station IDs: GL-047, GL-048, GL-052 on Commerce Ave). These have been out of service for 6 weeks. This is discouraging EV adoption in our area.
summary: 3 public EV chargers offline for 6 weeks on Commerce Ave, impacting EV adoption.
recommendation: DOT maintenance crew dispatched. Stations GL-047 and GL-048 repaired. GL-052 requires parts order (ETA 2 weeks). Service restoration notice sent to registered users.

text: The community garden on Elm Park has been requesting a composting station for 2 years. We generate significant organic waste and currently it all goes to landfill. Can we get connected to the city composting program?
summary: Elm Park Community Garden requesting composting program access for organic waste diversion.
recommendation: Add to commercial organics collection route. Provide 64-gallon organics bin and educational materials. Estimated diversion: 2.5 tons/year.

text: The solar installation on our school (Lincoln Elementary) has been pending permit approval for 4 months. This is a 45kW system that was donated through a community solar program. The delay is costing the school money and undermining trust in the program.
summary: 45kW donated solar installation at Lincoln Elementary delayed 4 months in permitting.
recommendation: Escalate to Solar Fast-Track desk. Policy §4.5 mandates 15-day approval for systems under the threshold. Review permit queue for other stalled community solar projects.

text: The stretch of Highland Avenue between 5th and 12th Street was supposed to get protected bike lanes this spring per the Bicycle Master Plan. Neighbors say the project was quietly removed from the capital plan. Can you confirm status?
summary: Residents questioning removal of planned protected bike lanes from capital plan on Highland Ave.
recommendation: DOT to provide written status update within 5 business days. If deferred, must demonstrate alternative timeline consistent with 200-mile 2031 target. Highland Ave is a high-injury corridor requiring Vision Zero intervention.

text: The old warehouse at 850 Industrial Blvd is being converted to lofts. Construction crews are leaving commercial dumpsters overflowing with materials, and most of it is recyclable lumber and drywall. This seems like a major waste compliance issue.
summary: Construction waste at 850 Industrial Blvd not being sorted for recycling/diversion.
recommendation: Request Construction & Demolition Waste Management Plan from contractor. Issue courtesy notice for materials separation. Recycling coordinator to offer on-site consultation.

text: My building's boiler is 30 years old and the landlord refuses to upgrade. We're in a rent-stabilized building at 412 Maple Street. Our heating bills are enormous and the building is drafty. Is there assistance available for tenants in this situation?
summary: Tenant at 412 Maple St seeking building retrofit assistance for aging boiler and poor insulation.
recommendation: Referred to Residential Climate Retrofit Fund. Income-qualified building may be eligible for up to $8,000 grant. Tenant advocacy referral provided. Building added to benchmarking priority list.

text: There's a large empty lot at the corner of River Road and 3rd Ave that floods every time it rains and sends runoff into the storm drain. Can this be converted to green infrastructure? It's been vacant for years.
summary: Vacant flood-prone lot at River Rd/3rd Ave identified as green infrastructure opportunity.
recommendation: Refer to Green Stormwater Infrastructure program. Site assessment for bioretention or pocket park conversion. Check ownership — if City-owned, fast-track for Tree Canopy and stormwater credit.

text: The city bus depot on South Street is running diesel buses 24/7 with engines idling for long periods in the neighborhood. Residents smell exhaust constantly. When is transit electrification coming to this facility?
summary: Excessive diesel idling at South Street bus depot impacting residential air quality.
recommendation: Fleet electrification plan for South Street depot scheduled for Phase 2 (2028). Immediate action: enforce 5-minute idling limit per §2.7. Install idle-reduction technology as interim measure. Community liaison meeting recommended.
